task_name: Add REST API Client Generator to Prisma

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here is the complete implementation plan for `packages/generator-rest-client/`.

---

## Plan Summary

### Files to create (7 new files)

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `packages/generator-rest-client/package.json` | `@prisma/generator-rest-client`, with `bin` pointing to `dist/index.js`, workspace deps on `generator-helper`, `generator`, `dmmf` |
| `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.json` | Extends `../../tsconfig.json` — identical to all other packages |
| `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.build.json` | Extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json`, `outDir: dist` |
| `packages/generator-rest-client/helpers/build.ts` | Spreads `adapterConfig` with `banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }` so `dist/index.js` is OS-executable |
| `packages/generator-rest-client/src/index.ts` | Calls `generatorHandler({ onManifest, onGenerate })` from `@prisma/generator-helper` |
| `packages/generator-rest-client/src/generator.ts` | Reads `options.dmmf.datamodel.models`, delegates to template, writes `rest-client.ts` via `fs/promises` |
| `packages/generator-rest-client/src/template.ts` | Pure functions: DMMF scalar→TS type mapping, per-model interface/input-type/client-class generation |

### File to modify (1)

- **`tsconfig.build.bundle.json`** — add `"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"]` to `compilerOptions.paths`

### Key design decisions

- Uses **`generatorHandler`** (community generator JSON-RPC protocol from `@prisma/generator-helper`) — simpler than the internal `Generator` class used by client generators
- Emits a **single `rest-client.ts`** file per run — no multi-file structure needed
- **Relation fields** (`kind === 'object'`) are skipped in all interfaces so REST types stay flat and serializable
- `src/template.ts` holds all string-generation logic (pure functions, easy to test in isolation)
- Each generated `*Client` class uses the native `fetch` API and follows REST conventions (`GET /users`, `POST /users`, `PATCH /users/:id`, `DELETE /users/:id`)
